title: galium commune subsp. jordanii
intro: Galium commune subsp. jordanii, also known as Jordan's bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the Middle East and North Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Jordan's bedstraw include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Galium commune subsp. jordanii can be found in various forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
